title: EEG: Three armed bandit gambling task
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from 23 healthy college students performing a three-armed bandit gambling task with dynamically oscillating reward probabilities. The task was designed to investigate neural correlates of reward processing and reward prediction error during adaptive decision-making and task set generation. Stimuli (blue, red, and green) had reinforcement probabilities that varied sinusoidally over trials, enabling examination of how the brain tracks and responds to changing contingencies.
methods_description: EEG data were collected from 23 healthy control subjects performing a three-armed bandit task implemented in MATLAB. The task involved three stimuli with oscillating reinforcement probabilities (ranging from 20% to 90%) that varied in phase across conditions. Corrugator EMG and skin conductance measurements were also recorded on most participants, though data quality was variable. Data collection occurred in 2014 at the Cognitive Rhythms and Computation Lab, University of New Mexico.
